yesterdayAnalysis: Solid 5.1-mile recovery run at HR 144, which is appropriate easy aerobic pace. This follows the 7.1-mile run on 5/12 (HR 149) and keeps run frequency high without overloading. The moderate heart rate suggests good aerobic efficiency and proper recovery between harder efforts.
todayRecommendation: Time for a brick workout to hit week 4's focus. Execute a 60-minute bike ride at race simulation intensity (145-152w NP) followed immediately by a 20-minute transition run. This is your key session to practice race pacing and neuromuscular adaptation.

Volume is strong but you need to execute this week's prescribed brick sessions and threshold swim work to stay on build phase trajectory.
keyInsight: Your conservative bike pacing strategy (66-69% FTP) is validated by the Oceanside 16-min run PR—protect this discipline in all brick work, especially with Oregon 70.3 just 65 days out.
